Regency at Fremont

CCN: 235176 · Fremont, MI 49412 · Newaygo County

Overview

Address
4554 West 48th Street, Fremont, MI 49412
Phone
2319243990
Certified beds
129
Avg daily residents
83 (64% of beds filled)
Ownership
For-profit corporation
Provider type
Medicare and Medicaid
Medicare/Medicaid since
1969-08-25
Setting
Rural
1 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 1/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 1/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 2/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 4/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 3/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 5/5

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 3.16 3.32 3.68 ≥ 3.48 Below floor
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.49 0.58 0.57 ≥ 0.55 Below floor
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 0.61 0.74 0.71 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 2.06 2.00 2.40 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.10 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.05 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
2.87
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.29
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
3.34
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
49.3%
annual
RN turnover
66.7%
annual
Administrators departed
1
last 12 months

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.18
higher acuity than avg
Case-Mix Index Ratio
0.85
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2025-04-10 13 13 0 80 2 120
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2024-04-18 15 8 7 120 2 180
Total weighted health score
135.0
lower is better

Deficiencies (31)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

30 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
1 G/H/I Actual harm
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. D 2025-04-10 2025-05-13
0557 Honor the resident's right to be treated with respect and dignity and to retain and use personal possessions. D 2025-04-10 2025-05-13
0558 Reasonably accommodate the needs and preferences of each resident. E 2025-04-10 2025-05-13
0565 Honor the resident's right to organize and participate in resident/family groups in the facility. E 2025-04-10 2025-05-13
0641 Ensure each resident receives an accurate assessment. D 2025-04-10 2025-05-13
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2025-04-10 2025-05-13
0658 Ensure services provided by the nursing facility meet professional standards of quality. E 2025-04-10 2025-06-11
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2025-04-10 2025-05-13
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. D 2025-04-10 2025-05-13
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. E 2025-04-10 2025-05-13
0756 Ensure a licensed pharmacist perform a monthly drug regimen review, including the medical chart, following irregularity reporting guidelines in developed policies and procedures. D 2025-04-10 2025-05-13
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. F 2025-04-10 2025-05-13
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2025-04-10 2025-05-13
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. D 2025-02-06 2025-03-12
0838 Conduct and document a facility-wide assessment to determine what resources are necessary to care for residents competently during both day-to-day operations (including nights and weekends) and emergencies. F 2025-02-06 2025-03-12
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2025-01-23 2025-02-18
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. D 2024-09-03 2024-09-26
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. E 2024-04-18 2024-07-10
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. D 2024-04-18 2024-05-14
0610 Respond appropriately to all alleged violations. D 2024-04-18 2024-05-14
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2024-04-18 2024-05-14
0725 Provide enough nursing staff every day to meet the needs of every resident; and have a licensed nurse in charge on each shift. E 2024-04-18 2024-07-10
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. D 2024-04-18 2024-05-14
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. F 2024-04-18 2024-05-14
0814 Dispose of garbage and refuse properly. F 2024-04-18 2024-05-14
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2024-02-22 2024-03-19
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. G 2023-08-16 2023-09-12
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. E 2023-05-25 2023-06-09
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. D 2023-05-25 2023-06-09
0908 Keep all essential equipment working safely. E 2023-05-25 2023-06-09
0624 Prepare residents for a safe transfer or discharge from the nursing home. D 2023-04-14 2023-05-01
